original: The new Winchester Cathedral is dedicated by bishop Walkelin in Winchester, England
Pope Eugene III takes refuge in the castle of Ptolemy II of Tusculum
Alexios III Angelos drives out his brother Isaac II as Byzantine emperor
Mongol army under General Subedei begins the siege of Chinese Jin capital of Kaifeng - 1st occasion gunpowder used in a major engagement
Krak des Chevaliers, the greatest fortress built by medieval crusaders in the Levant, is finally taken by the forces of Mamlūk sultan Baybars I
Petrarch crowned a poet on the Capitoline Hill in Rome
Bartolomeo Prignano is elected as Pope Urban VI
Portuguese sea captain Diogo Cão returns to Lisbon from his first voyage to explore the coast of Africa, during which he was the first European to discover the Congo River
Holy Roman Emperor convenes Imperial Diet in German city of Augsburg to address Protestant tensions
Congregation Shearith Israel opens the 1st North American synagogue in New York City on Mill Street in Lower Manhattan
British troops chase French out of Masulipatam, India
First fire escape is patented: a wicker basket on a pulley and chain
Ayutthaya Kingdom in present-day Thailand falls to Burmese invaders
Soldiers riot in Bucharest, kill 128 Jews
French Protestant church becomes state-supported & -controlled
Steamship "Great Western" makes her maiden voyage from Bristol, England, to New York
1st battle at Gioto: Sardinia-Piemonte beats Austrians
Battle at Xaquixaguana, Peru: Pedro de la Gasca beats Gonzalo Pizarro
US Civil War: US mint at Dahlonega, Georgia, seized by Confederacy
John D. Lynde patents aerosol dispenser
Battle of Mansfield, Louisiana; Federals routed by General Richard Taylor
Italy and Prussia ally against Austria-Hungary.
American Museum of Natural History opens in New York City
Amilcare Ponchielli's opera "La Gioconda" premieres in Milan
Khedive Ismael of Egypt fires French and British ministers
Milk sold in glass bottles for 1st time
The Critic reports that ice cream soda is America's national drink
Battle of Atbara River: Anglo-Egyptian forces defeat 15,000 Sudanese during the Mahdist War, a turning point in the reconquest of Sudan
Demonstration organised by socialists in Belgium as people demand better education, living conditions, the right to strike and universal male suffrage result in a riot and some deaths
Russia and China sign the Convention of Evacuation under which Russia agrees to evacuate Manchuria within 18 months
British mystic Aleister Crowley transcribes the first chapter of the Book of the Law
Great Britain and France establish their Entente Cordiale, a technical treaty settling long-standing disagreements over Morocco, Egypt, Africa, and the Pacific
New York City changes the name of Longacre Square to Times Square, in honor of The New York Times’s move to the area
1st race at the Playa Del Ray Motordrome, 1st US auto speedway, is held near Los Angeles, California
Steamers collide on the Nile, drowning 200
17th Amendment to the United States Constitution is ratified by Congress, providing for election of senators by popular vote
China's National Assembly opens in Peking, the first free democratic parliament in Chinese history
US & Colombia sign a treaty concerning Panama Canal Zone
Norway approves active & passive female suffrage
South African State pass the Industrial Conciliation Act No 11: provides for job reservation, excluded blacks from membership of registered trade unions, prohibited registration of black trade unions
"White Horse Inn" opens in London
Manchester Guardian warns of unknown Nazi terror
Works Progress Administration approved by Congress
King Zog I of Albania flees after Italy invades
German battle cruisers sink British aircraft carrier Glorious
1943 NFL Draft: Frank Sinkwich from University of Georgia first pick by Detroit Lions
Hakuun Yasutani Roshi, founder of Sanbo Kyodan, receives dharma
Stanley Cup Final, Boston Garden, Boston, MA: Detroit Red Wings beat Boston Bruins, 2-0 for a 4-0 series sweep and their 3rd SC Championship
1945 NFL Draft: Charley Trippi from University of Georgia first pick by Chicago Cardinals
Dutch Nazi occupiers executed, Nazi general Christiansen flees Netherlands
League of Nations assembles for the last time
Largest recorded sunspot ever observed at 40 times the diameter of Earth
Soen Nakagawa & Nyogen Senzaki (Zen teachers) meet in San Francisco
US President Harry Truman seizes steel mills to avert a strike
6 US Marine Corps recruits drown during a night "marsh march" in Ribbon Creek at Parris Island, South Carolina; drill instructor Staff Sergeant Matthew McKeon is court-martialed
Netherlands and Germany sign accord concerning war casualties
US Senate passes Civil Rights Bill with measures against discriminatory voting practices
British liner "Dara" explodes in Persian Gulf, kills 236
Accords of Evian (Algeria) accepted by referendum in France
Unmanned Gemini 1 launched
India & Pakistan forces engage in a border fight
OAO 1, 1st orbiting astronomical observatory, launched
Time publishes its "Is God Dead" issue - its first issue without an image
40th Academy Awards postponed to Apr 10th due to death of Martin Luther King
Czechoslovakia Oldřich Černík government forms
New socialist constitution of East Germany takes effect
WKPI TV channel 22 in Pikeville, Kentucky (PBS) begins broadcasting
Expansion teams Royals, Expos, Padres & Pilots win their 1st games
First Major League Baseball game featuring a Canadian team; Montréal Expos beats NY Mets, 11-10 at Shea Stadium; KC Royals, SD Padres and Seattle Pilots also win on debut
Senate rejects Nixon's nomination of Judge G. Harrold Carswell to US Supreme Court
1st legal off-track betting system begins (OTB-New York)
Alvin Kallicharran scores 100* in his 1st Test Cricket innings v NZ
Thirty-two terrorist bombings in Cyprus
Discovery Island opens at Walt Disney World, Florida
1976 NFL Draft: Lee Roy Selmon from University of Oklahoma first pick by Tampa Bay Buccaneers
205th & final episode of "All in the Family"; followed by "Archie Bunker's Place" for 4 seasons
People's Republic of China joins International Olympic Committee
Islander Potvin's 2 shorthanded goals tie NHL record vs Kings & set NHL rec of 2 shorthanded playoff goals in 1 period
Islanders scored 9 goals against Toronto in playoffs
Tracy Caulkins wins her 36th US swimming title aged 19
U.S. Census Bureau estimates rank Los Angeles as second most populated city, displacing Chicago which held the position since 1890; New York City remains the top
Amdahl releases UTS/V, the first mainframe Unix
India files suit against Union Carbide over Bhopal disaster
California Angels pitcher Jim Abbott, born without his right hand, makes MLB debut, lasting only 4 2/3 innings
King Birendra of Nepal lifts 30-year ban on political parties
Norwegian ferry Scandinavian Star catches fire; 159 people die
Political party New Democracy wins the national election in Greece
Major league umpires & baseball reach a 4-year agreement
Oakland A's stadium becomes 1st outdoor arena in USA to ban smoking
After 151 years Britain's "Punch Magazine" publishes its final issue
Indians' Carlos Baerga is 1st to switch hit HRs in same inn (vs Yanks)
STS-56 (Discovery) launches into orbit
Atlanta Brave Kent Mercker no-hits Dodgers, 6-0
Japanese Prime Minister Morihiro Hosokawa resigns
Smoking is banned in the Pentagon and all US military bases
American Bruce Seldon defeats by RTD over Tony Tucker in 7 rounds to win vacated WBA boxing title
BPAA US Open won by Dave Husted
Microsoft releases Internet Explorer 4 Beta
STS 83 (Columbia 22) lands
Indian political party Haryana Gana Parishad from the state of Haryana merges with the Indian National Congress
Nineteen US Marines are killed when a V-22 Osprey tilt-rotor aircraft crashes near Marana, Arizona
Humanitarian Ceasefire Agreement on the Conflict in Darfur is signed by the Sudanese government and two rebel groups, ending hostilities amid a 45-day ceasefire
Shedden massacre: The bodies of eight men, all shot to death, are found in a field in Ontario, Canada. The murders are soon linked to the Bandidos motorcycle gang.
World's first building to integrate wind turbines is completed in Bahrain
15 people are killed and 53 are wounded by a car bombing in Damascus
163 people are killed and 50,000 are displaced after tribal violence erupts in Darfur, Sudan
Filip Vujanović’s election as President of Montenegro is confirmed by the electoral commission
Fifth day of protests by thousands in Caracas, Venezuela against the government
1 in 4 Japanese adults a virgin aged 18-39 according to new research by Tokyo University
14 tons of black market Pangolin scales from 36,000 animals discovered in Singapore, one of largest ever found worldwide
600 million birds die each year in the US after striking tall buildings with Chicago the worst city, according to Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Actress Allison Mack pleads guilty to sex-trafficking charges for her involvement in sex cult NXIVM
Record 17ft (5.2M) invasive Burmese python pregnant with 73 eggs captured in Florida's Big Cypress National Preserve
Saudi-backed coalition fighting Houthi fighters in Yemen calls for a ceasefire after five years to stop the spread of COVID-19
World Trade organization predicts a drop in global trade greater than the 2008 financial crisis, between 13% and 32% for 2020
Egyptian archaeologists announce their most important find since Tutankhamun's tomb: the discovery of a lost 'golden city,' the 3,000-year-old ancient city of Aten near Luxor
Seventh night of unrest in Belfast, Northern Ireland even after joint call for calm by UK PM and Irish Premier
First all-private space flight, Axiom-1 launches to the International Space Station
Total solar eclipse stretches from Mazatlán, Mexico, to Newfoundland, plunging 44 million people into darkness
Jet Set nightclub roof collapse kills at least 221, during a performance by merengue singer Rubby Pérez and his orchestra, in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic
Roman EmperorCaracallais assassinated (and succeeded) by his Praetorian Guard prefect, Marcus Opellius Macrinus
Roger IIof Sicily is excommunicated
Alfonsode Borgia elected as Pope Callistus III
Battle of Novara: KingLouis XIIof France defeats DukeLudovico Sforzaof Milan
Premiere ofMozart's "Violin Sonata No. 27" in G major (K. 379)
Catherine IIof Russia annexes the Crimea
Carl Friedrich Gauss, German mathematician, proves the quadratic reciprocity law (the ability to determine the solvability of any quadratic equation in modular arithmetic)
Roman Catholic Diocese of Baltimore promoted to an archdiocese byPope Pius VII, with the founding of the dioceses of New York, Philadelphia, Boston, and Bardstown (now Louisville)
William Gladstoneintroduces the first Irish Home Rule Bill in the British House of Commons
H. H. AsquithsucceedsHenry Campbell-Bannermanas British Prime Minister
Indian Independence Movement: At the Delhi Central Assembly,Bhagat Singhand Batukeshwar Dutt throw handouts and bombs to court arrest
Dmitri Shostakovich's ballet "The Arrow" premieres
Béla Bartók's 5th String Quartet premieres in Washington, D.C.
Walter Piston's 1st Symphony in E, premieres in Boston, by the Boston Symphony, conducted by the composer
In his 4th title defense in 9 weeksJoe Louisbeats Tony Musto by TKO in the 9th round at the Arena, Saint Louis, Missouri to retain NYSAC heavyweight boxing crown
Arnold Schoenberg& Tudor's ballet "Pillar of Fire" premieres in NYC

U.S. PresidentFranklin Roosevelt, in an attempt to check inflation, freezes wages and prices, prohibits workers from changing jobs unless the war effort would be aided thereby, and bars rate increases to common carriers and public utilities.
Jomo Kenyattaconvicted of involvement with the Mau Mau rebellion and sentenced to 7 years jail in Kenya
35th Academy Awards: "Lawrence of Arabia",Anne Bancroft&Gregory Peckwin
Detroit Tigers claim young pitcherDenny McLainon waivers from the Chicago White Sox; McLain goes on the win Cy Young Award 1968, 69 and AL MVP 1968
American Football League votes in 36-year-oldAl Davisas commissioner after Joe Foss resigns; appointment lasts 3 months when AFL merges with NFL
Major League Baseball decides to postpone Opening Day because of the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr
Hammerin'Hank Aaronhits his 715th home run off LA Dodger Al Downing in Atlanta, breaking Babe Ruth's record
Paul McCartney& Wings release single "Band on the Run" in the US
47th Academy Awards: "The Godfather Part II",Ellen Burstyn&Art Carneywin
Frank Robinsondebuts as 1st African American baseball manager (Cleveland Indians beat New York Yankees, 5-3)
Israeli premierYitzhak Rabinresigns
Clint Eastwoodelected mayor of Carmel, California, makes his day
Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardois arrested in Mexico and charged with kidnapping, murder of DEA agent Enrique Camarena, drug trafficking and other crimes
"Twin Peaks" created byDavid Lynchand starring Kyle MacLachlan premieres on ABC-TV
Michael Landonannounces he has inoperable cancer of the pancreas
Retied American jockeyBill Shoemakerparalyzed in a car accident
MLB outfielderDarryl Strawberryenters Betty Ford Center in Rancho Mirage, California for substance abuse rehabilitation
Oliver McCall beatsLarry Holmesin 12 for heavyweight boxing title
Hear Music Records releases "So Beautiful or So What", the twelfth solo studio album by American singer-songwriterPaul Simon
German writerGünter Grasslabelled 'persona non grata' by Israeli internal affairs minister Eli Yishai, due to his poem "What Must Be Said"
PopeBenedict XVIcalls for an end to Syrian blood shed in papal Easter message
Mike Babcockwins his 414th career game as head coach of the Detroit Red Wings, becoming the winningest coach in Red Wings history (passing Jack Adams)
31st Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: Chicago, Cheap Trick, Deep Purple,Steve Miller, and N.W.A
Hungarian parliamentary election won by right-wing Fidesz–KDNP alliance withViktor Orbánremaining Prime Minister
Bernie Sandersdrops out of the Democratic race for US president
Dr.Anthony Faucithanks American health workers for their sacrifice during the pandemic, acknowledging their more than 3,600 deaths
US PresidentJoe Bidensays "Gun violence in this country is an epidemic" as he unveils package of executive actions including restrictions on "ghost guns"
Michael Bay's film "Ambulance" is released in the US by Universal Pictures
US Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences bansWill Smithfrom attending the Oscars for 10 years after he slapped hostChris Rockon stage during the 2022 ceremony
New Vatican document rejects concept of changing a person's biological sex despitePope Francis' recent overtures to the Trans community
The famous ancient Greek statue Venus de Milo is discovered on the Aegean island of Milos
In front of a live audience of 20 tourists,David Copperfieldmakes the Statue of Liberty disappear
U.S. National Security AdvisorCondoleezza Ricetestifies before the 9/11 Commission
Protests in Sudan against the government ofOmar al-Bashircontinue with seven killed and 2,500 arrested in Khartoum
